_comment: The ratchet. best[scenario] = highest weighted score that scenario has reached. iterations[] is the append-only ledger. A candidate merges only if aggregate improves AND no scenario < its best here.

iterations.diagnosis: Set-vs-session conflation: history.tsx appends one entry per completed SET, but HistoryHero counted history.length as sessions. One 3-set workout fabricated a '3 sessions logged' PR curve, defeating the MIN_SESSIONS=3 gate. Compounded across kgUnit/prHeavy/sparse/dense (inflated counts).
iterations.convergence: All 3 candidates independently found the same root cause.

winner.summary: Extracted inline useLiftSeries into pure RN-free buildLiftSeries; group history by calendar day so 1 session = 1 day; gate/count/curve on distinct days; added actionable empty state (progress pips toward the 3-session unlock); golden test.

winner.strategy: Reframe the WorkoutCard PR chip from 'ties the all-time max (>=)' to 'new all-time best at time logged' (strictly beats every prior training day). New RN-free buildPRDays(exerciseStats) collapses history to one day-bucket per training day, walks oldest->newest tracking a running max, and records each day that strictly exceeds it (first-ever day excluded). WorkoutCard now takes prDays and computes isPR via dayKeyOf(workout.createdAt).

Extract one clock-free, RN-free trend module (lib/history/exerciseTrend.ts) that collapses per-set history into best-per-training-day buckets via shared dayKeyOf, then derives BOTH ExerciseCard signals from the FULL logged window: delta = latest day-bucket best minus earliest (no 3-month cutoff), sparkline = last up-to-6 day-bucket bests gated to length>=2 (no live-clock calendar windows). ExerciseCard replaces getSparklineData/getDelta with one useMemo(computeExerciseTrend); history.tsx getImprovement ('Improved' sort) reuses the same module's e1rm variant, collapsing three divergent 'progress' defs into one. A metric param ('topWeight' default / 'e1rm' for sort) keeps both consumers exact. TREND_GOLDENS assert the extracted math across scenarios.

Extract deep-history plateau derivation into a pure, RN-free, clock-injectable module lib/history/prRecency.ts. computePRRecency(exerciseStats, now) reuses buildPRDays' day-bucket walk (dayKeyOf + e1rmLbs, first-day-excluded running-max) to return per-exercise lastPRDate, daysSincePR, sessionsSincePR, and gated isPlateau (daysSincePR>=21 AND sessionsSincePR>=4). HistoryHero titleLeft flips to a muted 'Plateau · N weeks since last PR' caption when the active lift is plateaued (dense: 131d -> ~19 weeks), keeping the celebratory gain when fresh (prHeavy last PR daysAgo(7) stays positive). Node-gateable: PR_RECENCY_GOLDENS + new correctness.test.ts describe assert against REFERENCE_NOW.
